topics.name: 1. Anthropic Cuts Off OpenClaw — The AI Platform War Heats Up
details: **What's happening:** Anthropic blocked third-party agent frameworks from using Claude subscriptions, starting with OpenClaw. Users now pay per-use or switch to API keys. Thousands of developers' workflows are broken overnight. Coverage across TNW, Digital Trends, Geeky Gadgets, Hacker News, beincrypto, and Bitcoin News (crypto agents heavily used OpenClaw).

**Why it matters for content:** This is a platform governance story that affects every AI builder. The "walled garden vs. open ecosystem" debate is back — except now it's about AI agents, not app stores.

**Suggested hook angle:** "Anthropic just broke thousands of AI automations overnight. Here's why — and what to do about it." Explain the OpenClaw situation, then show that native Claude Code skills are unaffected. Position yourself as the safe path.

topics.name: 2. The $1B One-Person Company Narrative Goes Mainstream
details: **What's happening:** Multiple viral X posts this week (@usmaanbuildsAI, @Sabrina_Ramonov, @ZavionDaDon, @raindog_kitetu) are all pushing the same stack: Claude Code + n8n + Stripe + AI agents = run a business solo. @raindog_kitetu claims $0 payroll, $1.14/month infra. The "solo AI founder" archetype is crystallizing.
**Why it matters for content:** This IS the CMNDSHFT pitch. Every one of these posts validates the "AI employees" positioning. The difference: Christian actually shows HOW, not just WHAT.

**Suggested hook angle:** "Everyone's posting their $0 AI stack. Here's what they're not telling you." Show the real complexity behind the viral tweets — the skills, the memory architecture, the edge cases. Honest take > hype take.

topics.name: 3. Claude Code Skills Marketplace Emerging
details: **What's happening:** Open-source skill packs for marketing, sales, SEO, and content are being shared across X and GitHub. @ihtesham2005's marketing agency skill drop (1,595 likes) keeps spreading. @ericosiu amplified it. @will_bender1 mapped 7 ways creators monetize Claude Code. Plugin executables (v2.1.92) now let skills ship binaries under bin/.
**Why it matters for content:** Skills are becoming the new "templates" — the currency of the AI creator economy. First movers who build reputation around high-quality skills win.

**Suggested hook angle:** "Claude Code skills are the new SaaS. Here's how to build and sell them." Walk through the skill structure, show a real skill being installed and run, then tease the CMNDSHFT skill giveaway funnel.

topics.name: 4. Claude Code Source Leak Aftermath Continues
details: **What's happening:** The March 31 source leak story (512K lines, 44 hidden feature flags, KAIROS daemon mode) continues to generate content. Users on r/ClaudeAI are still finding bugs in Anthropic's code and posting fixes. The "claw-code" clean-room rewrite hit 100K stars. Trojanized versions are still circulating (Help Net Security, The Register reporting malware angle).

**Why it matters for content:** The leak story is transitioning from "breaking news" to "analysis phase." There's still room for practical breakdowns, especially connecting leaked features to real workflows.

**Suggested hook angle:** "5 things from the Claude Code leak that actually matter for your workflow." Skip the drama, focus on what builders can use: KAIROS implications, the /dream memory system, and the feature flags that hint at what's shipping next.

topics.name: 5. AI Automation Backlash: "Got Fired for Automating My Job"
details: **What's happening:** The BORU (Best of Redditor Updates) post about someone being investigated for automating work hit 2,354 upvotes. The MIT "AI won't replace engineers" study generated a counter-narrative in comments (top comment: "We downsized by a third, everyone does 30% more with Claude Code"). IBM reportedly told managers to stop hiring. The tension between "AI won't replace you" and "AI already replaced your coworkers" is the conversation.
**Why it matters for content:** This is emotional content that drives shares. Real stories about real consequences of AI automation.

**Suggested hook angle:** "This guy got investigated for automating his own job. Here's what actually happened." Read the Reddit story on camera, then pivot: "The lesson isn't don't automate — it's automate correctly."

topics.name: - **v2.1.90**
details: **`/loop` gaining traction** — @JulianGoldieSEO: "Run once = automation. Run in a loop = system." The persistent agent use case is the next frontier.

ideas.title: Idea 1: "Anthropic Just Broke Thousands of AI Automations"

details.Title: Anthropic Killed OpenClaw Access — What It Means for Your AI Stack
details.Hook (spoken): "Anthropic just cut off 247,000 developers from using Claude through their favorite tool. If you're using AI automations, this affects you."
details.Text overlay: "CLAUDE JUST BLOCKED OPENCLAW — Are Your Automations Safe?"
details.Skill giveaway: Claude Code migration skill — moves any OpenClaw workflow into native Claude Code skills in 5 minutes
details.Why now: Story broke April 4, mainstream coverage is peaking TODAY. TNW, Digital Trends, Hacker News all running it. Developers are panicking and looking for alternatives. **This is a 24-hour window** before every tech YouTuber covers it.

details.Title: What They're NOT Telling You About the $0 AI Stack

details.Why now: 4+ viral X posts this week all pushing the solo AI founder stack. The trend is peaking. Christian can ride the wave while adding genuine substance — show the CLAUDE.md, the memory architecture, the skill dependencies that make it actually work.

ideas.title: Idea 4: "This Guy Got Investigated for Automating His Job"

details.Why now: The BORU post (2,354 upvotes) is still generating engagement. The MIT study comments are still being shared. IBM's "stop hiring" directive just leaked. The "should you automate quietly?" question is the water cooler conversation of the week.

ideas.title: Idea 5: "5 Hidden Features From the Claude Code Leak That Actually Matter"

details.Title: The Claude Code Leak Revealed 5 Features Nobody's Talking About
details.Hook (spoken): "Everyone covered the drama of Claude Code's source leak. Nobody covered the five features buried in the code that will change how you use it."
details.Text overlay: "5 HIDDEN FEATURES Found in Claude Code's Leaked Source"

details.Why now: The leak story is 5 days old, moving from "breaking news" to "analysis" phase. Most creators covered the drama (DMCA, malware). Almost nobody has done the practical "here's what you should actually care about" breakdown. The /dream feature (2,408 upvotes), KAIROS daemon mode, and the 44 feature flags are still under-covered from a builder's perspective.
